subject: Anthropology
title: Language Origins
description: A micro-lesson that makes Language Origins usable.

hook.text: No other species can say I love you, declare war, or tell a joke. Language is humanity's most powerful tool.

buildup.text: Language requires a descended larynx, brain regions like Broca's area, and the FOXP2 gene for fine motor control.

discovery.text: Anatomical evidence suggests full language capacity emerged by at least 100,000 years ago with modern Homo sapiens.

twist.text: Language may have evolved from gesture, song, or social grooming. The exact origin remains one of science's mysteries.

climax.text: Language let humans share knowledge across generations. It made culture cumulative — and that changed everything.

punchline.text: Words let us teach the unborn.

quiz.question: Why is language important to human evolution?
options: It made culture cumulative across generations
